name: Grilled Garden Pizza
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Quick oats or old-fashioned oats
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the warm water, active dry yeast, olive oil, and sugar in a mixing bowl. Let it stand for 10 minutes or until foamy.
2. In a separate large bowl, mix together the flour and oats. Gradually add the yeast mixture while stirring on low speed with an electric mixer for an additional 2 minutes. The dough will be soft.
3. Knead the dough on a floured surface five times. Place it in a medium-sized bowl sprayed with no-stick cooking spray, turning once to coat. Cover and let rise at room temperature for 30 minutes or until almost doubled.
4. Punch down the dough and divide into four portions. On a floured surface, pat each portion into a 6-inch circle.
5. Grill over medium hot coals for 2 to 4 minutes, or until the bottom is golden brown. Remove from grill.
6. On the bottom side, layer the remaining ingredients in the order listed: Romano cheese, half of the mozzarella, bell pepper, onion, garlic (minced), tomatoes, and basil.
7. Return the pizzas to the grill, cover, and cook for 4 to 6 minutes or until golden brown and the cheese begins to melt.
8. To bake in the oven, preheat to 425 degrees F. Spray a cookie sheet with no-stick cooking spray or oil lightly. Pat each portion of dough into a 6-inch circle on the prepared sheet.
9. Layer the Romano cheese, half of the mozzarella, bell pepper, onion, garlic (minced), tomatoes, and basil on top of each pizza. Bake for 25 minutes or until the crust is golden brown. Sprinkle with remaining cheese immediately.
